transformation_logic_plain: This column is a straight pass-through of the document date from the purchase order header, converted to a standard date format.
join_description: Header table — one row per PO
transformation_logic_plain_business: The date when the purchase order was created.

transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the vendor account number directly from SAP field EKKO.LIFNR without any modification through the data pipeline.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The unique identifier for the supplier who provides goods or services to the company.

transformation_logic_plain: Carries the GR posting date as recorded in MKPF.BUDAT, flowing through staging, vault, and mart layers unchanged to represent the date of the first goods receipt against a purchase order.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The date when goods were first physically received at the warehouse against a purchase order.

transformation_logic_plain: This column calculates the number of days between the purchase order date and the first goods receipt date for each purchase order, representing the actual vendor lead time.
transformation_logic_sql:
CASE WHEN fgr.first_gr_date IS NOT NULL AND ph.po_date IS NOT NULL
        THEN CAST(fgr.first_gr_date - ph.po_date AS INTEGER)
        ELSE NULL
    END
filter_description: Movement type 101 only — exclude reversals (102) and returns (122)
notes: Core calculation: date difference between PO and GR
transformation_logic_plain_business: The number of days it took for the supplier to deliver goods after the purchase order was placed.

transformation_logic_plain: This column converts SAP movement type codes into descriptive business categories, such as mapping code '101' to 'goods_receipt' and code '102' to 'gr_reversal', with any unmapped codes defaulting to 'other'.
transformation_logic_sql:
CASE
        WHEN gri.movement_type = '101' THEN 'goods_receipt'
        WHEN gri.movement_type = '102' THEN 'gr_reversal'
        WHEN gri.movement_type = '122' THEN 'vendor_return'
        WHEN gri.movement_type = '161' THEN 'customer_return'
        WHEN gri.movement_type = '201' THEN 'deployment'
        WHEN gri.movement_type = '202' THEN 'gi_reversal'
        WHEN gri.movement_type = '301' THEN 'plant_transfer'
        WHEN gri.movement_type = '561' THEN 'initial_stock'
        ELSE 'other'
    END
filter_description: Category 'vendor_return' isolates CPEs returned to the supplier — the numerator for cpe_defect_rate
transformation_logic_plain_business: The type of inventory movement that occurred, such as receiving goods from a supplier, returning items to a vendor, or transferring stock between locations.

transformation_logic_plain: This column calculates the number of days between the equipment's creation date and today's date, returning null if no creation date exists.
transformation_logic_sql:
CASE WHEN g.created_date IS NOT NULL
        THEN CAST(CURRENT_DATE - g.created_date AS INTEGER)
        ELSE NULL
    END
join_description: dim_equipment materializes days_since_receipt directly from EQUI
transformation_logic_plain_business: The number of days that have passed since goods were first received for this order.

transformation_logic_plain: This column represents the unrestricted stock quantity from the material master plant data, converted to a decimal format with 3 decimal places for precise inventory calculations.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The quantity of goods available for sale or use that are not reserved or restricted for specific purposes.

transformation_logic_plain: This column shows the positive quantity for goods receipts (movement types 101, 161, 202, 561) and negative quantity for goods issues (movement types 102, 122, 201), with all other movement types set to zero.
transformation_logic_sql:
CASE
        WHEN gri.movement_type IN ('101', '161', '202', '561') THEN gri.quantity
        WHEN gri.movement_type IN ('102', '122', '201') THEN -1 * gri.quantity
        ELSE 0
    END
filter_description: Movement type 201 = goods issue to cost center
transformation_logic_plain_business: The quantity of inventory added to or removed from stock, with positive numbers for goods received and negative numbers for goods returned or transferred out.

transformation_logic_plain: The total stock is the sum of unrestricted stock, quality inspection stock, and blocked stock from the material master, converted to a decimal format.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The total quantity of materials currently available in the warehouse, including both unrestricted stock and quality inspection stock.

transformation_logic_plain: The column represents the average quantity of goods issued to cost centers (movement type 201) across all relevant transactions.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The average amount of goods moved into inventory during stock receipts.

transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the equipment number directly from SAP field EQUI.EQUNR through all pipeline layers unchanged to serve as the primary identifier for equipment records.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The unique identifier that tracks each piece of equipment throughout its entire lifecycle.

transformation_logic_plain: The lifecycle_status column maps SAP equipment status codes to business-friendly labels, such as 'INST' to 'deployed' and 'AVLB' to 'in_stock', with any unmapped codes defaulting to 'unknown'.
transformation_logic_sql:
CASE
        WHEN ls.status_code = 'INST' THEN 'deployed'
        WHEN ls.status_code = 'AVLB' THEN 'in_stock'
        WHEN ls.status_code = 'RET' THEN 'returned'
        WHEN ls.status_code = 'DLFL' THEN 'defective'
        ELSE 'unknown'
    END
transformation_logic_plain_business: The current status of the equipment showing whether it's actively deployed, available in inventory, returned from use, or defective.

transformation_logic_plain: This column contains the purchase requisition date from the SAP purchase requisition document, converted to a standard date format.
filter_description: Only PRs that converted to POs
transformation_logic_plain_business: The date when the purchase request was first submitted for approval.

transformation_logic_plain: The number of days between the purchase requisition date and the purchase order creation date, calculated only when both dates are available.
transformation_logic_sql:
CASE WHEN prd.requisition_date IS NOT NULL AND ph.po_date IS NOT NULL
        THEN CAST(ph.po_date - prd.requisition_date AS INTEGER)
        ELSE NULL
    END
notes: MRP-generated PRs are auto-converted and skew the metric
transformation_logic_plain_business: The number of days between when goods were requested and when the purchase order was created.

transformation_logic_plain: This column calculates each vendor's share of total quarterly spend as a percentage, dividing the vendor's quarterly spending by the organization's total quarterly spending and rounding to four decimal places.
transformation_logic_sql:
CASE WHEN qt.quarter_total_spend > 0
        THEN ROUND(vq.total_spend / qt.quarter_total_spend, 4)
        ELSE 0
    END
transformation_logic_plain_business: The percentage of total company spending that went to this specific supplier during the quarter.

transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the purchase order document number as a direct copy of the SAP EKKO.EBELN field, flowing through unchanged from the source system.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The unique identifier assigned to each purchase order when it is created in the system.

transformation_logic_plain: This column represents the total net value of all purchase order line items by summing the net worth amounts from the purchasing document items.
join_description: PO item grain — multiple items per PO
transformation_logic_plain_business: The total monetary value of all items purchased in this purchase order.

transformation_logic_plain: This column categorizes each purchase order line item's delivery status as "pending" when no goods receipts exist, "fully_received" when the total received quantity equals or exceeds the ordered quantity, or "partially_received" when some but not all ordered quantity has been received.
transformation_logic_sql:
CASE
        WHEN fgr.first_gr_date IS NULL THEN 'pending'
        WHEN gr_totals.total_gr_quantity >= pi.ordered_quantity THEN 'fully_received'
        WHEN gr_totals.total_gr_quantity > 0 THEN 'partially_received'
        ELSE 'pending'
    END
transformation_logic_plain_business: Shows whether ordered items have been fully delivered, partially delivered, or are still pending receipt at the warehouse.

source_description: GR posted quantity from PO history
transformation_logic_plain: This column shows the total quantity of goods received for each purchase order, defaulting to zero when no goods receipt records exist.
join_description: EKBE one row per GR event
transformation_logic_plain_business: The total quantity of goods that have been received from the supplier for this delivery.

transformation_logic_plain: This column calculates each vendor's percentage share of total quarterly spend by dividing the vendor's quarterly spend by the overall quarterly spend total, rounded to four decimal places, or zero if there was no total spend in the quarter.
transformation_logic_sql:
CASE WHEN qt.quarter_total_spend > 0
        THEN ROUND(vq.total_spend / qt.quarter_total_spend, 4)
        ELSE 0
    END
join_description: Join EKKO to EKPO on EBELN
transformation_logic_plain_business: The percentage of total company spending that went to this specific supplier during the quarter.

transformation_logic_plain: The fulfillment rate represents the percentage of ordered quantity that was actually received from the vendor, calculated by dividing total received quantity by total ordered quantity and rounded to four decimal places, or zero if no quantity was ordered.
transformation_logic_sql:
CASE WHEN vq.total_ordered_qty > 0
        THEN ROUND(vq.total_received_qty / vq.total_ordered_qty, 4)
        ELSE 0
    END
transformation_logic_plain_business: The percentage of ordered goods that the vendor actually delivered, showing how reliable they are at fulfilling orders completely.

transformation_logic_plain: The vendor performance grade assigns letter grades based on the vendor's on-time delivery rate, where vendors with 80% or higher on-time delivery receive an 'A', 60-79% receive a 'B', 40-59% receive a 'C', and below 40% receive a 'D'.
transformation_logic_sql:
CASE
        WHEN AVG(CASE WHEN f.is_on_time THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) >= 0.80 THEN 'A'
        WHEN AVG(CASE WHEN f.is_on_time THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) >= 0.60 THEN 'B'
        WHEN AVG(CASE WHEN f.is_on_time THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) >= 0.40 THEN 'C'
        ELSE 'D'
    END
transformation_logic_plain_business: A letter grade (A, B, C, or D) that rates how reliably a supplier delivers goods on time, with A being the best performers.

transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the equipment number that flows through unchanged from the SAP EQUI.EQUNR field, identifying each serial-tracked CPE device in the system.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The unique identifier assigned to each individual piece of customer equipment being tracked in the system.

transformation_logic_plain: The deployment rate percentage is calculated by dividing the count of equipment with 'deployed' lifecycle status by the total count of all equipment records, then multiplying by 100 and rounding to one decimal place.
transformation_logic_sql: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN e.lifecycle_status = 'deployed' THEN 1 END) / COUNT(*), 1)
transformation_logic_plain_business: The percentage of equipment items that have been successfully deployed and are currently in active use.

transformation_logic_plain: The return rate percentage is calculated by dividing the number of items with lifecycle status 'returned' by the total number of items and multiplying by 100, rounded to two decimal places.
transformation_logic_sql: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN e.lifecycle_status = 'returned' THEN 1 END) / COUNT(*), 2)
transformation_logic_plain_business: The percentage of equipment items that were returned back to the supplier out of all equipment received.

transformation_logic_plain: The total stock is the sum of unrestricted stock, quality inspection stock, and blocked stock quantities, converted to a decimal format with three decimal places.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The total quantity of items available across all warehouse locations, including goods ready for use, under inspection, and temporarily blocked.

transformation_logic_plain: This flag indicates whether a storage location has zero or negative unrestricted stock quantity.
transformation_logic_sql: CASE WHEN sl.unrestricted_stock <= 0 THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END
transformation_logic_plain_business: Indicates whether a warehouse location has completely run out of available inventory.

transformation_logic_plain: The blocked stock represents the total quantity of materials that are blocked from use, summed across all plant-material combinations from the MARD table's blocked stock field.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The total quantity of inventory items that are temporarily unavailable for use due to quality issues or other restrictions.

transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the material document number that flows through unchanged from the SAP MSEG.MBLNR field, serving as the unique identifier for each goods movement transaction.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The unique reference number that identifies each individual goods movement transaction in the system.

transformation_logic_plain: This ratio compares the total quantity of goods returned from deployment (movement type 161) to the total quantity of goods issued to deployment (movement type 201).
transformation_logic_sql: SUM(qty WHERE bwart='161') / SUM(qty WHERE bwart='201')
transformation_logic_plain_business: The ratio of goods returned to suppliers versus goods issued to production, indicating how often deployed materials come back versus go out.

transformation_logic_plain: The column counts the number of unique purchase orders created in each year-month period, derived by grouping purchase order document numbers by the first 7 characters of the document date (YYYY-MM format).
transformation_logic_plain_business: The year and month when purchase orders were created, used to track ordering activity over time.

transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the vendor account number directly from the SAP EKKO.LIFNR field, flowing through unchanged to identify which vendor is associated with each three-way match variance record.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The supplier who provided the goods or services for this purchase order invoice variance.

transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the purchase order quantity directly from the SAP field EKPO.MENGE, flowing through unchanged to support three-way matching variance analysis.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The quantity of items originally ordered from the supplier on the purchase order.

transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the goods receipt quantity directly from the SAP EKBE.MENGE field, flowing through unchanged from the purchase order history.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The quantity of goods that was actually received and recorded when the delivery arrived.

transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the invoice document number directly from SAP field RBKP.BELNR, flowing through unchanged to identify invoices in three-way match variance analysis.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The unique identifier for each invoice document that helps track billing discrepancies.

transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the invoice line quantity directly from SAP field RSEG.MENGE, flowing through unchanged from source to support three-way match variance analysis.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The quantity of goods or services listed on the supplier's invoice for this line item.

transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the vendor name as a direct copy of the LFA1.NAME1 field from SAP, flowing through unchanged to support three-way match variance analysis.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The name of the supplier that sent the invoice being compared against the purchase order and goods receipt.

transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the approval workflow state directly from the SAP ZMM_APPROVAL_LOG.APPR_STATUS field, flowing through unchanged to track purchase order invoice three-way match variance approval status.
filter_description: APPR_STATUS IN ('02','03','04') for all exceptions; '02' for approved
transformation_logic_plain_business: The number of invoice payment exceptions that have been approved despite discrepancies between the purchase order, goods receipt, and invoice amounts.

transformation_logic_plain: This column represents the scheduled delivery date from the purchase order schedule line, converted from the original SAP date format to a standard date format.
filter_description: Latest schedule line per PO item
transformation_logic_plain_business: The planned date when goods were expected to arrive from the supplier.

transformation_logic_plain: This column indicates whether a purchase order was delivered on time by comparing the first goods receipt date against the scheduled delivery date plus a 2-day grace period, returning true if delivered within that window and null if either date is missing.
transformation_logic_sql:
CASE
        WHEN fgr.first_gr_date IS NOT NULL AND ps.scheduled_delivery_date IS NOT NULL
        THEN CASE WHEN fgr.first_gr_date <= ps.scheduled_delivery_date + INTERVAL 2 DAY
                  THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END
        ELSE NULL
    END
notes: Tolerance approved by Head of Supply Chain
transformation_logic_plain_business: Shows whether goods were delivered within 2 days of the promised delivery date.

source_description: Material document line items — MSEG contains each goods receipt line with MENGE quantity, BWART movement type to filter for receipts (101), and BUDAT posting date for monthly aggregation.
transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the raw quantity values from SAP field MSEG.MENGE, flowing through unchanged from the material document line items table.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The quantity of goods received from suppliers each month.

source_description: Material document headers — MKPF provides document-level context and BUDAT posting date for temporal grouping, joined to MSEG via MBLNR.
transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the material document number from SAP field MKPF.MBLNR, flowing through unchanged to enable goods receipt tracking and aggregation by month.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The unique reference number that identifies each goods receipt transaction for monthly reporting and tracking purposes.

source_description: Material Document Number linking to goods receipt header
transformation_logic_plain: Counts the total number of goods receipt line items recorded in the period to give the monthly receipt volume.
join_description: Join MSEG to MKPF on MBLNR for document date aggregation
filter_description: Filter BWART = '101' for goods receipts into warehouse stock
transformation_logic_plain_business: The total number of individual goods receipt line items recorded in a given month.

transformation_logic_plain: Truncates the goods receipt posting date to the first day of its calendar month, grouping all receipts within the same month together for monthly aggregation.
join_description: Header table for document date information
transformation_logic_plain_business: The calendar month in which goods were received and recorded, used to group and compare receipt activity over time.

source_description: Equipment master — provides equipment details and installation status for CPE devices.
transformation_logic_plain: Carries the count of active and deployed CPE devices as a direct pass-through of EQUI.EQUNR from the SAP Equipment master, flowing unchanged through staging, vault, and mart layers.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The number of customer premises equipment (CPE) devices that are currently active and installed at customer locations.

source_description: Material document header — provides posting dates for goods movements to determine current status.
transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the material document number from SAP field MKPF.MBLNR, flowing through the staging, vault, and mart layers unchanged to reflect the count of currently active and deployed CPE units based on goods movement postings.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The total number of Customer Premises Equipment (CPE) units that are currently active and in use at customer locations.

source_description: Material movement lines — contains BWART movement types to classify deployment vs warehouse vs return movements.
transformation_logic_plain: Carries the SAP material movement type directly from MSEG.BWART, flowing unchanged through all pipeline layers to classify each movement line as a deployment, warehouse, or return transaction for active CPE unit counting.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The movement classification code that identifies whether an active piece of customer premises equipment was deployed to a customer, stored in a warehouse, or returned — used to count how many units are currently active in the field.

source_description: Equipment object list — bridges equipment to serial numbers for per-serial tracking.
transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the count of actively deployed customer premises equipment and flows through unchanged from SAP field OBJK.SERNR, which bridges equipment records to their serial numbers for individual unit tracking.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The number of customer premises equipment units (such as routers or modems) that are currently active and installed at customer locations.

source_description: Serial number master — provides serial number details and links to goods movement documents.
transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the serial number value flowing through unchanged from the SAP field SERI.SERNR, representing the count of active deployed CPE units identified by their serial number records.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The number of active Customer Premises Equipment (CPE) units currently deployed and in use at customer locations, tracked by their unique serial numbers.

source_description: Billing document header — provides FKSTO (cancellation flag; filter FKSTO='' to exclude cancelled documents per business_filter_description), FKDAT (billing date used as the basis for MIN(FKDAT) per customer to establish the tenure anchor), KUNAG (sold-to party linking to KNA1 for secondary segment attributes), and WAERK (currency). Revenue grain is monthly by billing date.
transformation_logic_plain: Carries the cancellation status flag directly from SAP field FKSTO on the VBRK billing document header, flowing through staging, vault, and mart layers unchanged.
transformation_logic_plain_business: Whether this billing document has been cancelled, as recorded directly from the original invoice header.

source_description: Billing document line — provides NETWR (net billed service revenue in EUR, the primary revenue measure), MATNR (service-plan material for CPE filter and service_plan segmentation), MATKL (material group for CPE-% filter), AUBEL/AUPOS (reference back to originating sales order, enabling join to VBAK/VBAP for service plan and device link). Joins to VBRK on VBELN.
transformation_logic_plain: Carries the net billed service revenue in EUR directly from SAP field VBRP.NETWR through staging, vault, and mart layers unchanged as the primary revenue measure underpinning contribution margin calculations by segment.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The total revenue billed to customers in euros for each service, used as the starting point for calculating how much profit each business segment generates.

source_description: Sales order header — provides KUNNR (customer link to KNA1 for secondary segments KTOKD and ORT01) and AUART/SPART/VTWEG for service plan classification. Needed because VBRP.AUBEL references the sales order number; VBAK bridges the billing line to the customer master.
transformation_logic_plain: Carries the sales order number directly from VBAK.VBELN, flowing unchanged through staging, vault, and mart layers to link each billing line to its originating sales order header and associated customer, service plan, and segment attributes.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The unique identifier that links each billing charge back to the original sales order it came from, allowing revenue and margin to be traced to the correct customer, service plan, and business segment.

source_description: Sales order line item — provides MATKL (material group for CPE-% filter), MATNR (service-plan material number driving fiber/TV/cable/business segmentation), and SERNR (serial number of the deployed CPE device). VBAP.SERNR is the entry point into the per-device amortization chain: VBAP.SERNR → SERI.SERNR → SERI.EQUNR → EQUI.INBDT.
transformation_logic_plain: Carries the sales order line item's material number directly from VBAP.MATNR, flowing unchanged through staging, vault, and mart layers to drive fiber, TV, cable, and business segment classification for CPE contribution margin reporting.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The product identifier assigned to each sales order line, used to categorize items into fiber, TV, cable, or business segments for contribution margin reporting.

source_description: Customer master — retained in scope for the secondary segment dimensions only: KTOKD (customer account group: residential/business) and ORT01 (city, 12-city secondary dimension). Tenure is no longer derived from KNA1.ERDAT; instead tenure = months since MIN(VBRK.FKDAT) per KUNAG.
transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the customer account group classification (residential or business) flowing through unchanged from SAP field KNA1.KTOKD, used exclusively to support secondary segment dimension filtering within the contribution margin by segment reporting structure.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The customer category indicating whether the account belongs to a residential or business customer, used to filter and compare profitability across different customer segments.

source_description: Purchase order header — provides EBELN (PO number) and WAERS (currency) as the header anchor for the device unit cost chain (EKKO → EKPO). Required to filter CPE procurement documents and carry PO-level attributes to the line cost calculation.
transformation_logic_plain: Carries the purchase order number directly from SAP field EKKO.EBELN, flowing through staging, vault, and mart layers unchanged as the header anchor identifier for CPE contribution margin calculations by segment.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The unique reference number assigned to each purchase order, used to link and calculate profit contribution across different business segments.

source_description: Purchase order line — provides NETPR (net price per unit, the authoritative device unit purchase cost for straight-line amortization over 24 months per analyst decision #3) and MATKL (CPE material group filter). NETPR is the sole cost numerator in the amortization formula: amortized_monthly_cost = NETPR / 24.
transformation_logic_plain: Carries the net price per unit sourced directly from SAP field NETPR (EKPO), flowing through staging, vault, and mart layers unchanged as the authoritative device unit purchase cost used in contribution margin by segment calculations.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The purchase price paid per unit of a device, used to calculate how much profit each product segment contributes after costs.

source_description: Vendor invoice header — provides BELNR (invoice document number), BUDAT (invoice posting date), and LIFNR (vendor); needed to identify posted vendor invoices per the business_filter_description ('posted vendor invoices only'). Also provides the header-level posted status used to filter out unposted or parked invoices.
transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the vendor invoice document number (BELNR) from RBKP unchanged through all pipeline layers into the contribution margin by segment fact table.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The unique reference number assigned to the supplier's invoice, used to trace billing back to the original document.

source_description: Vendor invoice line — provides EBELN/EBELP (links to PO line, confirming which PO line items have been invoiced and are therefore 'posted') and MATNR (for CPE material filter at invoice line level). Note: per analyst decision #3, RSEG.WRBTR is NOT used as the device cost basis; EKPO.NETPR is used instead. RSEG remains in scope to identify which PO lines have confirmed posted invoices.
transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the vendor invoice line amount directly from SAP field RSEG.WRBTR, flowing through staging, vault, and mart layers unchanged to confirm which purchase order lines have posted invoices linked to CPE materials.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The billed amount from the supplier's invoice for a specific purchase order line, used to identify which ordered items have been invoiced and are linked to CPE materials.

source_description: Material master — provides MATKL (material group for 'MATKL LIKE CPE-%' filter) and MTART (material type) to restrict scope to CPE materials across all procurement, billing, and movement legs. Serves as the shared CPE-filter anchor across the cost, revenue, and returns sub-queries.
transformation_logic_plain: Carries the material group value directly from MARA.MATKL, flowing through staging, vault, and mart layers unchanged to anchor the CPE material scope filter across all cost, revenue, and returns segments.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The product category used to group materials into the correct business segment when calculating contribution margins across costs, revenue, and returns.

source_description: Equipment master — provides INBDT (date put into service / installation date), which is the deployment start date for the 24-month straight-line amortization window per analyst decision #2. A device is within its amortization window in month M if INBDT <= M < INBDT + 24 months. Joins to SERI via EQUNR.
transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the equipment installation date directly from SAP field INBDT (EQUI) — the date a device was put into service — flowing through staging, vault, and mart layers unchanged.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The date when the equipment was officially installed and put into service at the customer's location.

source_description: Serial number record — provides the per-unit link from a deployed CPE device's serial number (SERNR, joinable from VBAP.SERNR) to the equipment master (EQUNR → EQUI.INBDT). Per analyst decision #4, SERI is used exclusively in the chain VBAP.SERNR → SERI.SERNR → SERI.EQUNR → EQUI.INBDT. The prior SERI → MSEG join (MBLNR/ZEILE) is removed because cardinality evidence showed catastrophic_fanout (DAR-00539) or no_signal (DAR-00579), making it an invalid per-device join key.
transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the equipment number directly from SAP field SERI.EQUNR, flowing unchanged through staging, vault, and mart layers as the per-unit link connecting a deployed CPE device's serial number to its equipment master record.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The unique identifier assigned to each customer premises equipment (CPE) device, used to track that specific unit throughout its lifecycle.

source_description: Material document line — retained in scope ONLY for returns cost calculation per analyst decision #4: BWART (movement type; filter to 161/122 for return movements), DMBTR (amount in local currency for returns valuation), and MATNR (CPE material filter). Returns cost is aggregated at material/movement level (not joined per-device to SERI). Joins to MKPF for posting date.
transformation_logic_plain: Carries the material movement type directly from SAP field MSEG.BWART, flowing through staging, vault, and mart layers unchanged to support filtering of return movements (161/122) in the returns cost calculation underlying contribution margin by segment.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The category of stock movement (such as a return or a receipt) used to identify and separate returned goods when calculating the profit contribution for each business segment.

source_description: Material document header — provides BUDAT (goods movement posting date) for the returns leg; required as the header for MSEG to get the authoritative posting date for return events. Used solely in the returns cost aggregation sub-query (MSEG BWART 161/122), not in the per-device amortization path.
transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the goods movement posting date directly from SAP field MKPF.BUDAT, flowing through staging, vault, and mart layers unchanged as the authoritative posting date for customer returns events used in the returns cost aggregation.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The date when a customer return was officially recorded in the system, used to calculate the costs associated with that return.

source_description: Carries order_purchase_timestamp (month dimension) and order_status (needed to filter out canceled orders); grain anchor for the non-canceled order filter.
transformation_logic_plain: Carries the order purchase timestamp directly from ORDERS.ORDER_PURCHASE_TIMESTAMP through staging, vault, and mart layers unchanged, serving as the month-level grain anchor used to attribute gross merchandise value to its corresponding category and period.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The date and time when a customer placed their order, used to group sales revenue by month and product category.

source_description: Carries price (the BRL measure, explicitly excluding freight per definition) and product_id (FK to products); one row per item, summed to produce GMV.
transformation_logic_plain: Carries the per-item transaction price directly from the SAP field ORDER_ITEMS.PRICE, flowing through staging, vault, and mart layers unchanged to support monthly gross merchandise value aggregation by category.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The selling price of each individual item at the time it was ordered, used to calculate total sales revenue by product category each month.

source_description: Carries product_category_name (Portuguese) and product_id; the LEFT JOIN source that enables the 'uncategorized' grouping when product_category_name IS NULL.
transformation_logic_plain: Carries the product category name directly from SAP field PRODUCT_CATEGORY_NAME through all pipeline layers unchanged, enabling grouping of sales by category — including an 'uncategorized' bucket when no category is assigned.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The name of the product category used to group and total sales revenue each month, shown as 'uncategorized' when a product has not been assigned to a category.

source_description: Translates product_category_name (Portuguese) to product_category_name_english; required by the definition's 'per English product category' grain.
transformation_logic_plain: Carries the English-language product category name directly from SAP field CATEGORY_TRANSLATION.PRODUCT_CATEGORY_NAME_ENGLISH, enabling gross merchandise value reporting at the per-English-product-category grain.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The English name of the product category used to group and report total sales value each month.

source_description: Fact table carrying one row per order with order_purchase_timestamp (used to derive the month grain) and customer_id (FK to customers); provides the denominator (all orders) and the date dimension for monthly bucketing.
transformation_logic_plain: Carries the original order purchase timestamp directly from the SAP field ORDER_PURCHASE_TIMESTAMP, flowing through staging, vault, and mart layers unchanged to support monthly bucketing and repeat customer rate calculations.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The date and time when a customer originally placed their order, used to identify whether they are a returning customer within a given month.

source_description: Carries customer_unique_id — the stable person-level identifier required by the definition to distinguish the same person across multiple orders; customer_id is order-scoped and cannot be used for repeat-customer detection without this table.
transformation_logic_plain: Carries the stable person-level identifier sourced directly from SAP field CUSTOMER_UNIQUE_ID, flowing through staging, vault, and mart layers unchanged to support repeat-customer detection across multiple orders.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The unique identifier assigned to each customer that allows the business to recognize when the same person has placed more than one order.

source_description: Purchase timestamp (when the order was placed)
transformation_logic_plain: Truncates the order purchase timestamp to the first day of its calendar month, grouping all orders into monthly periods for aggregation.
join_description: orders is joined to order_items on order_id (header_detail, safe direction order_items->orders per DAR-00962, avg 1.14 items/order). orders is used solely to supply the purchase timestamp and the cancellation filter — it does not drive the aggregation grain.
filter_description: WHERE order_status != 'canceled' — confirmed valid by DAR-00973 code_tables analysis (625 canceled rows out of 99,441 total; order_status has zero nulls per DAR-00970)
transformation_logic_plain_business: The calendar month in which customer orders were placed, used to group sales activity by month.

transformation_logic_plain: The total gross merchandise value in BRL for each category and month, calculated by summing the price of all order items within that grouping.
join_description: order_items is the fact grain. Each row is one item line. Joined to orders on order_id to apply the status filter (header_detail per DAR-00962). Joined to products on product_id (safe direction order_items->products, catastrophic fanout warning applies only to reverse direction per DAR-00963).
filter_description: Inherit the order_status != 'canceled' filter from orders join. No additional price filter — zero nulls confirmed.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The total sales value in Brazilian Reais generated within each product category for the month.

source_description: English category name — target label for grouping
transformation_logic_plain: The English product category name sourced from the category translation reference table.
join_description: Resolved via products LEFT JOIN category_translation on product_category_name. The join key in category_translation has 0 nulls and 73 distinct values (DAR-00993). All 73 Portuguese keys match at 1.0 ratio (DAR-00958).
filter_description: No filter — COALESCE handles the NULL case for uncategorized products.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The product category name in English that groups items sold, used to analyze revenue performance across different types of products.

source_description: Unique identifier of the order — used as the unit of counting for numerator and denominator
transformation_logic_plain: Counts the total number of unique orders placed, used as the basis for calculating the repeat customer rate.
join_description: Primary table; one row per order. Joined to customers via customer_id (per_record_key, avg 1.00x per DAR-00959)
filter_description: Exclude canceled orders (order_status NOT IN ('canceled','unavailable')). See warning on analyst filter decision blocker.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The total number of individual orders placed by customers who have purchased more than once.

source_description: Purchase timestamp — used to derive order month and to determine order sequence per person
transformation_logic_plain: The order month is taken directly from the order purchase timestamp field as recorded in the orders data.
join_description: Column on orders; no additional join needed
filter_description: Zero nulls confirmed (DAR-00931: null_pct=0.0, spans 2016-09-04 to 2018-10-17). No COALESCE needed.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The calendar month in which customer orders were placed, used to track how often existing customers return to make repeat purchases over time.

source_description: Orders table carries order_purchase_timestamp (the 'month of order purchase' grain dimension), order_id (the unit being counted as repeat or not), and customer_id (the FK used to join to customers for person-level identity resolution).
transformation_logic_plain: This column carries the repeat_customer_rate_blind value as a direct copy of the SAP field ORDERS.ORDER_PURCHASE_TIMESTAMP, flowing the order purchase timestamp grain unchanged through staging, vault, and mart layers to support month-level identification of customers placing more than one order.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The percentage of customers who placed more than one order within a given month, calculated using the date and time each order was made.

source_description: Customers table carries customer_unique_id — the stable person-level identifier explicitly described in the catalog as 'use this to find repeat customers across orders', distinguishing it from customer_id which is unique per order. This field is the only available mechanism to group multiple orders to a single person and therefore determine whether any given order belongs to a repeat customer.
transformation_logic_plain: Carries the stable person-level identifier from SAP field CUSTOMER_UNIQUE_ID, flowing through unchanged from the CUSTOMERS table to support identification of repeat customers across multiple orders.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The unique identifier assigned to each customer, used to recognize when the same person has placed more than one order over time.

source_description: Single source of all required fields: order_id (grain key), order_purchase_timestamp (month bucketing), order_status (filter to 'delivered'), order_delivered_customer_date (actual delivery date, numerator condition), and order_estimated_delivery_date (promised date at purchase, comparison target). The term definition is fully self-contained within this table — no joins are required.
transformation_logic_plain: Carries the raw order identifier from SAP field ORDERS.ORDER_ID as a direct pass-through through all pipeline layers, serving as the grain key that anchors each record used to calculate on-time delivery rate from order status, actual delivery date, and estimated delivery date within the same table.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The unique reference number assigned to each customer order, used to track whether that order was delivered on time based on its actual versus expected delivery date.

source_description: Purchase timestamp; zero nulls, spans 2016-09-04 to 2018-10-17 (DAR-01024). Monthly grain derived via DATE_TRUNC.
transformation_logic_plain: Truncates the order purchase timestamp to the first day of its calendar month, grouping orders into monthly buckets for on-time delivery analysis.
filter_description: Not filtered; used as GROUP BY key
transformation_logic_plain_business: The calendar month in which the customer placed their order, used to group and analyze delivery performance over time.

transformation_logic_plain: Counts the total number of line items included in the monthly GMV aggregation for each category.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The total number of individual items sold within each product category during the month.

transformation_logic_plain: Counts the number of unique orders included in each monthly category record.
transformation_logic_plain_business: The total number of unique customer orders placed within each product category for the month.

transformation_logic_plain: The percentage of all distinct orders that were placed by repeat customers, calculated by dividing the count of repeat orders by the total count of orders and multiplying by 100, rounded to two decimal places.
transformation_logic_sql:
ROUND(
        100.0
        * COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN is_repeat_order THEN order_id END)
        / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT order_id), 0)
    , 2)
transformation_logic_plain_business: The percentage of all orders that were placed by customers who have ordered more than once, showing how well the business retains and re-engages its customers.

transformation_logic_plain: Counts the number of distinct orders that are flagged as repeat orders (i.e., placed by a customer who has ordered more than once).
transformation_logic_sql: COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN is_repeat_order THEN order_id END)
transformation_logic_plain_business: The number of orders placed by customers who have purchased from the business before.

transformation_logic_plain: Counts the total number of delivered orders recorded for the month.
notes: Backfilled from column lineage after the numeric-literal origin fix (review round 3).
transformation_logic_plain_business: The total number of customer orders that were successfully delivered during the measured period.

transformation_logic_plain: Counts the total number of orders that were delivered to the customer on time within each monthly period.
notes: Backfilled from column lineage after the numeric-literal origin fix (review round 3).
transformation_logic_plain_business: The total number of customer orders that were delivered on or before the promised delivery date.

transformation_logic_plain: Calculates the percentage of orders delivered on time within each monthly period by dividing the count of on-time deliveries by the total number of orders, expressed as a percentage rounded to four decimal places.
notes: Backfilled from column lineage after the numeric-literal origin fix (review round 3).
transformation_logic_plain_business: The percentage of customer orders that were delivered on or before the promised delivery date.
